channel.title: Journey on Здесь будет название
channel.description: Recent content in Journey on Здесь будет название

item.title: A Daughter of the Snows
item.description: Frona Welse was a daughter of the snows, born and raised in the harsh beauty of the Yukon. While other women of her generation learned needlework and piano, she learned to drive a dog team and shoot a rifle. The wilderness was her classroom, and it had taught her well.

item.title: The Sun-Dog Trail
item.description:
The sun-dog trail stretched across the frozen landscape, named for the optical phenomena that appeared on bright winter days — false suns flanking the real one, created by ice crystals in the atmosphere. The old-timers took sun dogs as a sign, though they disagreed about what the sign meant.

Sitka Charley was running the mail that winter, a hundred pounds of letters and packages destined for the mining camps along the Yukon. He was a native man, one of the few trusted with this crucial work, his knowledge of the land unmatched by any white man in the territory.

item.title: Love of Life
item.description: Love of life — it was stronger than he had ever imagined, this desperate will to continue existing. He had thought he was ready to die. He had made his peace, said his goodbyes to the world. But when death approached, something primal within him rose up in furious resistance.

item.title: The One Thousand Dozen
item.description: David Rasmunsen had a plan — a brilliant plan, he thought, that would make him rich without the backbreaking labor of mining. He would transport one thousand dozen eggs to Dawson City, where fresh eggs sold for outrageous prices. The math was simple: buy low in San Francisco, sell high in the Klondike, and pocket the difference.

item.title: The Gold Fever
item.description: The gold fever swept through the nation like a plague in 1897. Ships returned from Alaska carrying men who had been poor and were now wealthy beyond imagination. Newspapers printed their pictures alongside stories of nuggets the size of eggs, of claims that yielded thousands of dollars a day, of fortunes made overnight.

item.title: For the Love of a Man
item.description:
For the love of a man, Buck performed what might be considered a miracle. John Thornton saved Buck’s life, and in return, Buck gave him something that could not be measured — a devotion so complete, so absolute, that it transcended the ordinary bonds between man and dog.

They had found each other at the lowest point of Buck’s existence. The previous owners, Hal, Charles, and Mercedes, had driven the team to the breaking point. Dogs had died on the trail, worn out by overwork and underfeeding. Buck himself had been near death when Thornton intervened.

item.title: The Toil of Trace and Trail
item.description: The toil of trace and trail shaped them all — men and dogs alike. Day after day, they pushed forward through the frozen wilderness, carrying the mail that connected the isolated communities of the North. It was brutal work, but there was a strange beauty in it, a rhythm that became almost meditative.

item.title: Into the Primitive
item.description: Old longings nomadic leap, chafing at custom’s chain; Again from its brumal sleep wakens the ferine strain. These words echoed through Buck’s consciousness as he journeyed deeper into the primitive world of the North. Each mile traveled was a mile further from civilization, a mile closer to something ancient and untamed.
